stemHtml: The difference between the effects of slat and flap asymmetry is that: ("large" in the context of this question means not or hardly controllable by normal use of controls)

options.a: flap asymmetry causes a large yawing moment whereas slat asymmetry causes a large rolling moment at any speed.
options.b: flap asymmetry causes a large rolling moment whereas slat asymmetry causes a large yawing moment.
options.c: flap asymmetry causes a large difference in C<sub>LMAX</sub> whereas slat asymmetry causes a large rolling moment at any speed.
options.d: flap asymmetry causes a large rolling moment at any speed whereas slat asymmetry causes a large difference in C<sub>LMAX</sub>.
